summary: Iteration 11: Tested new upload endpoints, content_type support, and frontend UI elements for image upload and content type selection. All 14 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified: Admin Generator shows content type selector (Articolo/Landing/Pillar) with WP post/page distinction. Client Generator shows Note aggiuntive textarea and Immagini upload section with 'Formato orizzontale' note.
passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/uploads with .txt file returns 400 'Formato non supportato'
Backend: POST /api/uploads with valid JPG returns {id, path, filename, size}
Backend: POST /api/articles/generate-and-publish accepts all content_type values
Frontend Admin: Step 5 Articolo Singolo shows 'Tipo contenuto' selector with 3 buttons (Articolo, Landing Page, Pillar Page)
Frontend Admin: Landing/Pillar selection shows 'Verra pubblicato come Pagina su WordPress' message
Frontend Admin: Articolo selection shows 'Verra pubblicato come Post su WordPress' message
Frontend Admin: Step 5 SEO Programmatica shows 'Tipo contenuto' dropdown in Riepilogo card
Frontend Client: After analysis shows 'Note aggiuntive' textarea
Frontend Client: Shows 'Formato orizzontale consigliato. JPG, PNG o WebP, max 5MB.' note
Frontend Client: File input accepts image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp

upload_endpoint.POST /api/uploads: Returns {id, path, filename, size} for valid JPG
upload_endpoint.GET /api/uploads/files/{id}: Returns image binary with correct Content-Type

content_type_field.wp_type_mapping: articolo -> WP Post, landing_page/pillar_page -> WP Page

admin_ui_content_type.wp_message: Shows Post/Page distinction based on selection

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 11 verified all upload and content_type features. Upload endpoint works with object storage integration. Content type selector in Admin Generator properly shows 3 options and differentiates between WP Post and Page. Client Generator shows image upload section with correct format notes. File input accepts JPG/PNG/WebP. Evidenza badge and remove button functionality exist in code but were not tested E2E due to storage integration complexity. All backend tests pass 100%.
